top_categories.workout_context: Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 52 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
52 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
76ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Base fitness for Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
At Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,986ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: August in Rena to Lillehammer, Norway can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel’s 52 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Birkebeinerrittet Sykkel requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 52 miles of mixed terrain in Rena to Lillehammer, Norway.
